channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Two Individuals Booked Under NSA Following Noida Workers’ Protest Turning Violent
item.description: In Noida, Uttar Pradesh, the police have registered cases under the National Security Act (NSA) against two individuals for allegedly inciting violence during workers’ protests in April. The protests,...

item.title: House Ethics Committee investigates GOP Congressman for alleged sexual harassment
item.description: The House Ethics Committee has officially launched an investigation into Republican Congressman Chuck Edwards of North Carolina following allegations of sexual harassment. Reports indicate that Edward...

item.title: Wuffles Joint Chews Improve Mobility and Comfort in Senior Dogs
item.description: Senior dogs often face challenges with mobility, stiffness, and joint pain, making daily activities and playtime difficult. Wuffles hip and joint chews, currently discounted during Amazon Pet Days, ha...

item.title: Tanzania Discusses Public-Private Partnerships to Boost National Development
item.description: On 9th March 2026, the Public-Private Partnership Center (PPPC) of Tanzania organised a major conference at the University of Dar es Salaam, gathering government officials, private sector players, aca...

item.title: Progressive Alliance Parties Walk Out of Parliament After Motion of No Confidence Against Ramaphosa Denied
item.description: On Thursday, members of the Progressive Alliance, including the EFF and MK Party, staged a walkout in the National Assembly after Speaker Thoko Didiza declined their request to debate a motion of no c...

item.title: Niklaus Stoecklin and His Legacy in Swiss Poster Art
item.description: Niklaus Stoecklin (1896–1982) was a pivotal figure in 20th-century Swiss art, renowned for his contributions to the New Objectivity (Neue Sachlichkeit) movement and modern poster design. His work is d...

item.title: Upperlink joins Nigeria Revenue Service to support e-invoicing and digital tax compliance
item.description: Nigeria don dey push make tax system and business invoicing full digital, and Upperlink Limited don get green light from Nigeria Revenue Service (NRS) to act as System Integrator for the national e-In...

item.title: Angus Taylor outlines plans to reduce government size, cut taxes and tighten immigration
item.description: In his budget-in-reply speech, Opposition Leader Angus Taylor committed to significantly downsizing government and restricting publicly-funded services to Australian citizens. He proposed abolishing s...

item.title: Calgary Welcomes the Return of Fairy Tales Film Festival Showcasing International LGBTQ+ Stories
item.description: The Calgary Fairy Tales Film Festival is returning this year, bringing with it a diverse selection of international queer films and engaging community discussions. The festival, a staple in Calgary's ...

item.title: MEA Confirms Safety of Crew After Attack on Indian-flagged Ship Near Oman
item.description: The Indian government on Thursday strongly condemned an attack on an Indian-flagged vessel off the coast of Oman, calling the incident 'unacceptable'. Fortunately, all crew members on board were repor...

item.title: Sarafina! Revival Brings Soweto Uprising Memories to Life
item.description: The iconic musical 'Sarafina!' has returned to its birthplace at the Gibson Kente Theatre in Soweto, marking 50 years since the historic 1976 Soweto Uprising. Directed by Mpho Molepo, this 2026 reviva...

item.title: Doris Day’s Lifelong Dedication to Animal Welfare
item.description: Doris Day, renowned actress and singer, was equally famous for her deep love and dedication to animals. Her passion began after the loss of her childhood dog, Tiny, which inspired her lifelong mission...

item.title: Trump Administration Subpoenas Wall Street Journal and Other Outlets Seeking Sources on Iran War Reporting
item.description: The US Justice Department, under President Donald Trump's administration, has issued grand jury subpoenas to The Wall Street Journal and other major news organizations regarding their coverage of the ...

item.title: Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan holds initial meeting on NEET re-exam following paper leak
item.description: The Ministry of Education, led by Dharmendra Pradhan, convened its first meeting to discuss the possibility of conducting a re-examination for NEET after reports of a paper leak emerged. The meeting b...

item.title: Potential Impacts of a Strong El Niño on Canadian Weather
item.description: Scientists are predicting the possible emergence of a strong or 'super' El Niño in the coming months, which could have significant impacts on weather patterns in Canada. El Niño, a natural climate phe...

item.title: AFP Chief Warns of Political and Information Threats Amid West Philippine Sea Tensions
item.description: Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) Chief of Staff, Gen. Romeo Brawner Jr., has urged Filipinos to adopt a wartime mindset as the country faces political, economic, and information warfare linked to...

item.title: Tinubu Urges Unity and Fair Play as APC Primaries Begin
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don call on All Progressives Congress (APC) members make dem put unity and sportsmanship first as di nationwide primaries don start. Di primaries dey start from House of Represen...

item.title: Medical plane crash in New Mexico kills four and triggers wildfire
item.description: A small medical plane crashed in a mountainous region near Ruidoso, New Mexico, before dawn on Thursday, resulting in the deaths of all four people aboard. The victims, identified as flight crew and m...

item.title: Australian high jumper Erin Shaw begins recovery after spinal injury
item.description: Australian high jump prodigy Erin Shaw is embarking on an inspiring journey to recovery following a severe spinal injury sustained in a gym accident. The incident, described as horrific, left Shaw wit...

item.title: Chief Justice encourages Tanzanians to transform skills into income sources
item.description: The Chief Justice (CJ) of Tanzania has called upon citizens, especially the youth, to identify and utilise their talents as a way to generate income and improve livelihoods. During a recent statement,...
